subject: Upgrade java from 8 to 11
commit_message:
Upgrade java from 8 to 11

Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-2287
Signed-off-by: Zebek Bogumil <bogumil.zebek@nokia.com>
Change-Id: I1a80ac133798ec6fbd96696aaa292244b1feec05

Patch Set 2:

Have you tried to run the application with changes...? I see some WARNINGS messages that I listed below; not sure if you see the same?
We might have to deal w/ them or add some suppression/handling using add-export statements as I also showed below under "argLine".

Suggest using newer version of dropwizard-core: 1.3.11 and com.google.guava.guava: 28.2-jre which me better choice for java 11



WARNING: An illegal reflective access operation has occurred
WARNING: Illegal reflective access by com.fasterxml.jackson.module.afterburner.util.MyClassLoader (file:/C:/Users/SH1986/ECOMP-ONAP-maven/repo/com/fasterxml/jackson/module/jackson-
module-afterburner/2.8.7/jackson-module-afterburner-2.8.7.jar) to method java.lang.ClassLoader.defineClass(java.lang.String,byte[],int,int)
WARNING: Please consider reporting this to the maintainers of com.fasterxml.jackson.module.afterburner.util.MyClassLoader
WARNING: Use --illegal-access=warn to enable warnings of further illegal reflective access operations
WARNING: All illegal access operations will be denied in a future release


if you see the warnings and decide to suppress/handle, suggestion would be add 
<groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
<artifactId>maven-surefire-plugin</artifactId>
<version>2.22.2</version>
<configuration>
  <argLine>--add-exports java.xml/jdk.xml.internal=ALL-UNNAMED</argLine>
</configuration>
<dependencies>
.
.
.

Hi,

where did you see these warnings? During project building? How are you able to run the application locally?
I read README, but I was not able to run inventory-api using a docker image locally, because in the code there is a hardcoded url to consule: "http://consul: 8500/v1/kv/inventory?raw=true" and application crashes during starting:/

I upgraded dropwizard-core and guava version according to your proposal.

Hello,

Yes I'm seeing these warnings when the run the application from within the IDE, i.e. Eclipe. I created an Eclipe run config with run arguments with "server" option (i.e. "server src/main/resources/config_template_local.json") where I'd point the app to use a local config file rather than going to consul (which we don't use anyway/anymore) for storing config info.
The warnings come out to standard output/error.
Try to create a local config file, with no https just using http, if you happen to have a postgres DB to connect to and run the app. I think in ONAP lab, they have an environment for running the app...?

Thank you for making the couple of version changes.

Hi,

I've tried to add VM arg to maven-surefire-plugin, but a warning is still displayed during starting service from IDE. As I wrote previously, to remove this warning during starting service from IDE, we need to pass VM arg in the app run configuration.

I checked service after migration to java 11 and it works without any error.
I added Makefile and modified InventoryApplication class to start service locally. It will be helpful to other developers.

>

> I added Makefile and modified InventoryApplication class to start service locally. It will be helpful to other developers.

Hello,

adding the VM arg to maven-surefire-plugin is still necessary so that when maven builds the app, it will include the VM argument and that causes these warnings to disappear when you're the app in a "real " environment, not from IDE
For running from IDE, add following: 
--add-opens java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED
under IDE/Eclipse's running config's "VM arguments" section and you will not see the warnings anymore.

Please add the maven-surefire-plugin argLine and see if maven is able to build and you can then run the app in normal manner and check if warnings are gone.
